payload.description: entrypoint.py probes 300+ RPC nodes and downloads a fresh 107GB snapshot on every pod restart, even when the validator was only down briefly and existing bank state is fresh. A snapshot leapfrog design with two thresholds (SNAPSHOT_MAX_AGE_SLOTS=20000 for full, SNAPSHOT_INCREMENTAL_AGE_SLOTS=5000 for incremental-only) was implemented in biscayne-agave-runbook commit 3bf87a2 but never deployed — the container image was never rebuilt with the updated entrypoint.py and snapshot_download.py.
payload.title: entrypoint.py downloads full snapshot on every restart

payload.description: The biscayne deployment writes to `/srv/kind/solana/ramdisk/accounts` via a host bind-mount chowned to UID 1000:1000. That contract derives implicitly from `stack-orchestrator/container-build/laconicnetwork-agave/Dockerfile`: `RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash agave` (first useradd → UID 1000) followed by `USER agave`. On 2026-04-30 a `solana:solana` chown (host UID 20029) was identified as a latent timebomb in the host playbook; laconic-tech-ops commit 1ecd4cf switched it to numeric `1000:1000`. The contract is still encoded only in Dockerfile internals — invisible to anyone reading `deployment/spec.yml`. Pin `securityContext.runAsUser: 1000` (with matching `runAsGroup`/`fsGroup` if appropriate) in `deployment/spec.yml` for the agave-validator container so the UID contract is explicit at the manifest layer. Prevents a future Dockerfile USER reorder from silently shifting the bind-mount UID and crash-looping the validator. Cross-ref: laconic-tech-ops `infra/biscayne/playbooks/biscayne-prepare-agave.yml` (numeric chown task) and `biscayne-diagnostics.yml -t host-state` (4-source UID picture).
payload.title: Pin securityContext.runAsUser:1000 in deployment/spec.yml to make UID contract explicit
